title: 🤖 How I Vibe Code with AI (Even When It Goes Haywire)
author: FrivXd
text:
🤖 How I Vibe Code with AI (Even When It Goes Haywire)
The App we be building
So we will be building a compliments app like what it will do is take the mood and users image and just compliment him based on the mood user selected and image user shared, we will also have a feed public page and leaderboards
Stack we be using
we will be using, not me alone me and ai of course,
next js , supabase, vercel ai sdk and shadcn etc
My way to vibe the code
so i first go to my chatgpt to find some idea, like which catches my attention and is somewhat easy to make,
i tell it to do this sometimes
“i need you to give me a list of some ugly useless apps we can make but somehow they have potential to get hit online, like those apps don’t server much of a purpose but have potential etc etc”
this is as simple as i go
then it just throws some stuff at me and is like here you go damn guy just spits it out doesn’t even thing that it might overwhelm me
this what it throws at me…
1. “How Long Can You Hold?”
A button on screen. The longer you hold it, the higher your score. Global leaderboard.
Bonus: Daily/Weekly “holding champion”.
Why it might hit: It’s a challenge. It’s absurd. People love breaking records.2. Mood Detector (Lie Edition)User presses a button and it shows random mood: “You’re angry”, “You’re romantic”. UI is ugly but dramatic. Maybe retro or comic-sans everywhere.
Why it might hit: Fake AI “diagnosis” of mood is funny and shareable.3. The Pointless Button
Press a button. It makes random sound effects or weird messages like “Nice press”.
Bonus: 0 functionality. Just a button. Maybe unlocks “gold button” after 1,000 presses.
Why it might hit: Anti-productivity humor is gold. Irony = virality.continue….
so then what i do is just narrow it down
tell it to choose the three most convincing to you and then from those three i take my thing and get cooking…
give me 3 which u rate good and a bit more functionality to it like we can use some ai into it as well
this simple but works..
then we go to architecture building stuff
then i describe it the features and also tell it to come up with some features or make enhancements to min then it does that and boom somehow we are on the same page
just speak the THANG no need to type cool shi..
then i just dumb it to v0…
this thing is heaven for frontend devs, just post the screenshots and see the magic roll out fr…
after some prompt fights like
no do this man..
yeah this no no no, ohh god no
we are here
i told you its good… even the functionality half worked i could have just build my whole thing in this but no credits man i’m poooor.
so next step is to migrate this v0 stuff to cursor where we will integrate everything with supabase the all in one backend solution, it’s freaking cool to work with
after migration i setup model context protocol in short MCP in my codebase
📦 MCP Setup
so i go to cursor setup my supabase mcp
in .cursor directory inside that mcp.json file
you can visit https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/mcp?queryGroups=os&os=windows to setup yours and also get the api key from supabase settings
after that i setup the rules
🧱 Cursor Rules
rules are important it gives like some starter context or starter realization like what it has to do or what pattern it has to follow for the whole cycle of your app
so you setup rules in your .cursor directory create a folder rules inside it and add the .mdc files with the correct description of your rules, pattern
tasks etc u want cursor ai to follow or do.
mostly what i do is for supabase auth or queries etc stuff it it will use from mcp i just use the rules provided by supabase
mcp is powerful using that cursor can do anything in supabase crud, storage setups edge function etc etc it is diabolically strong using mcp
https://supabase.com/ui/docs/ai-editors-rules/prompts
check this directory as well alot of mcp related stuff here as well https://cursor.directory/rules/supabase
so these rules are limited to your project i think there is a way to setup em up globally as well… below the global setup
🌐 Global Rules in Cursor Setup
now these are set globally means it will work for any codebase
📓 global.md
for Tracking
I keep a global.md
in the root:
- Tracks everything done so far
- Acts like a changelog
- Cursor is set to update this file after every task
- Cursor also reads it before doing anything new
This keeps everything in sync, even when things get messy.
poolen file xD.. this is from a another project this should be global.md
after that i get to vibe coding yahooo the real game starts
lemme release this prompt on cursor u will pay for this cursor i had to still write so much…
then it starts cooking
and 1,2,…3 here we go
🎉 THE MVP TADAAA…
check the working thang here…
https://compliments-lilac.vercel.app/
💭 Final Thoughts
Sometimes the process is clean. Most of the time? It’s chaos.
But as long as you:
- Know your codebase
- Stick to your architecture
- Use tools like Cursor, Supabase, and GPT wisely
You’ll build fast, learn faster, and maybe even ship something that randomly goes viral.
Just vibe it. No cool quotes. No overthinking. Just build the dang thing.
signing out… good bye
